back_translation: There was a certain man from Ramathaim-zophim, from the hill country of Ephraim, and his name was Elkanah son of Jeroham son of Elihu son of Tohu son of Zuph, an Ephraimite.
He had two wives — the name of one was Hannah, and the name of the second was Peninnah. Peninnah had children, but Hannah had no children.
This man went up from his city from year to year to worship and sacrifice to the LORD of Hosts at Shiloh, and there the two sons of Eli — Hophni and Phinehas — were priests to the LORD.
On the day that Elkanah offered his sacrifice, he gave to Peninnah his wife and to all her sons and daughters portions.
But to Hannah he gave a double portion, because he loved Hannah — though the LORD had closed her womb.
Her rival provoked her sorely also, to make her grieve, because the LORD had closed her womb.
And so it was done year after year — as often as she went up to the house of the LORD, so she would provoke her, and she wept and would not eat.
Elkanah her husband said to her, 'Hannah, why do you weep, and why do you not eat, and why is your heart grieved? Am I not better to you than ten sons?'
Hannah rose after eating in Shiloh and after drinking, and Eli the priest was sitting on the chair by the doorpost of the temple of the LORD.
She was bitter of soul, and she prayed to the LORD and wept greatly.

She vowed a vow and said, 'O LORD of Hosts, if You will surely look upon the affliction of Your maidservant and remember me, and not forget Your maidservant, but give to Your maidservant a seed of men — I will give him to the LORD all the days of his life, and a razor shall not go up upon his head.'
It was as she multiplied her praying before the LORD, that Eli was watching her mouth.
Hannah was speaking upon her heart — only her lips were moving, but her voice was not heard. So Eli thought her a drunken woman.
Eli said to her, 'How long will you be drunk? Put away your wine from upon you.'
Hannah answered and said, 'No, my lord — a woman heavy of spirit am I, and wine and strong drink I have not drunk, but I have poured out my soul before the LORD.'
'Do not give your maidservant before a daughter of Belial, for out of the abundance of my complaint and my vexation I have spoken until now.'
Eli answered and said, 'Go for peace, and the God of Israel will grant your petition which you have asked from him.'
She said, 'May your maidservant find favor in your eyes.' And the woman went on her way and ate, and her face was no longer hers.
They rose early in the morning and worshiped before the LORD, and they returned and came to their house at Ramah, and Elkanah knew Hannah his wife, and the LORD remembered her.
It was at the turning of the days, that Hannah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Samuel, 'because from the LORD I have asked him.'
The man Elkanah went up, and all his house, to sacrifice to the LORD the sacrifice of the days and his vow.
But Hannah did not go up, for she said to her husband, 'Until the child is weaned, then I will bring him and he shall appear before the LORD, and dwell there forever.'
Elkanah her husband said to her, 'Do what is good in your eyes — stay until you have weaned him; only may the LORD establish his word.' So the woman stayed and nursed her son until she weaned him.

She brought him up with her as she weaned him, with bulls three (a three-year-old bull), and one ephah of flour, and a skin of wine, and she brought him to the house of the LORD at Shiloh — and the child was young.
They slaughtered the bull and brought the child to Eli.
She said, 'Please, my lord, by the life of your soul, my lord — I am the woman who stood with you in this place to pray to the LORD.'
'For this child I prayed, and the LORD has given me my petition which I asked from him.'
'And I also have lent him to the LORD — all the days that he lives, he is the one asked for the LORD.' And he worshiped there to the LORD.
